title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Jenny explains to Hercule Poirot the elaborate plan she and Richard Nigges devised to frame Nancy Duane for a series of murders. The group, consisting of Jenny, Richard, Harriet Sipprell, and Ada Gransbury, agreed to a sequence of mutual executions at a London hotel to make the deaths appear to be the work of a single killer. The intended order of the killings was for Ada to kill Harriet, Richard to kill Ada, and Jenny to kill Richard. To mislead the police, each murderer would place a cufflink engraved with the initials of Patrick Ives in the victim's mouth and arrange the scene to mimic previous deaths. They also planned to use blood from a stray cat and a note from an accomplice, Semple, to stage Jenny's own death, making it appear as though Nancy had killed all four of them for revenge. Semple was tasked with planting the victims' keys in Nancy's possession to ensure her conviction.

Jenny reveals there were two versions of the plan: one known to Harriet and Ada, and a secret version known only to Richard and Jenny. While the others believed the goal was a collective execution, Jenny's true motive was to survive to witness Nancy's hanging, after which Jenny would either commit suicide or kill Nancy if the execution failed. Richard had left the final decision regarding Nancy's fate to Jenny, believing her to be the most capable. However, Jenny confesses to Poirot that she is paralyzed by a fear of death and the act of killing, which has complicated the execution of their scheme.
